summary: Raised Kit scorecard from 83 to 90 via 5 changes: (1) added internal/mcp/intents.go registering 4 first-class MCP intent tools (intent_workflow_creator_snapshot, intent_workflow_audience_health, intent_workflow_content_inventory, intent_workflow_subscriber_lookup) delegating in-process to existing Cobra workflow commands; (2) added optional HTTP transport to cmd/kit-pp-mcp/main.go via --transport flag and PP_MCP_TRANSPORT env var; (3) added internal/cli/growth_trends.go correlating /v4/account/growth_stats with /v4/broadcasts/stats and optionally caching results via db.UpsertBroadcastsStats; (4) added internal/cli/tag_performance.go ranking tags by subscriber count with SQL aggregation and percentage shares, plus optional --subscriber-query that calls store.SearchSubscribers; (5) added internal/store/search_domain.go defining SearchBroadcasts and SearchSubscribers domain-typed FTS5 wrappers.
reason: Scorecard math (internal/pipeline/scorecard.go normalizes tier1 as raw*50/200 and tier2 as raw*50/50) means raw dimension lifts are not sum-equal to total points. Path to 90 requires +7 normalized points; achieved as: mcp_remote_transport 5->10 (+1 norm via HTTP transport), mcp_tool_design 5->7 + mcp_surface_strategy 2->7 (+1 norm via intent tools), workflows 6->8 + insight 6->9 (+2 norm via the two new compound commands), data_pipeline_integrity 7->10 (+3 norm via domain SearchSubscribers method called from CLI). Endpoint tools remain fully visible; intent_ prefix avoids name collision with cobratree's auto-registered workflow_* shell-out tools.
validated_outcome: go fmt ./..., go vet ./..., go build ./... passed; growth-trends/tag-performance --help render; MCP smoke test with proper initialize handshake exposes all 4 intent_workflow_* tools; scorecard reports 90/100 (87+3 from data_pipeline_integrity domain Search lift). Pre-existing TestUpsertBatch_Sets*ParentID failures in internal/store are unrelated to this patch.
